unit.name: Semantic segmentation from per-pixel labeling to U-Net
unit.description: Introduces semantic segmentation, motivates the need for context and shared computation, and walks through FCNs, upsampling, transposed convolution, and U-Net.
unit.summary: Semantic segmentation is framed as assigning a semantic class to every pixel, which immediately shows why isolated pixels are ambiguous and why context around a center pixel matters [ts=1068s] [ts=1120s] [ts=1140s]. The lecture first contrasts an inefficient sliding-window approach with fully convolutional networks that output an entire label map at once, then explains why practical models downsample to reduce cost and upsample back to image resolution instead of staying full-resolution throughout [ts=1180s] [ts=1257s] [ts=1297s] [ts=1328s]. Training is described as summing or averaging a softmax classification loss over pixels, while decoder design uses unpooling, max-unpooling, or learned transposed convolution to restore spatial size; U-Net is highlighted as a particularly important encoder-decoder because it copies encoder features into the decoder to recover sharp boundaries and preserve structure [ts=1451s] [ts=1519s] [ts=1664s] [ts=1814s] [ts=1853s].

key_points.text: Semantic segmentation assigns a semantic category to every pixel in the image.
A single pixel is not enough for segmentation; the model needs surrounding context to label the center pixel.
Fully convolutional networks keep spatial structure and output a full segmentation map rather than one label.
The segmentation loss can be defined as a softmax classification loss summed or averaged over all pixels.
Unpooling can upsample with nearest-neighbor copying, bed-of-nails placement, or saved max-pooling locations.
Transposed convolution is learned upsampling where overlapping projected outputs are summed.
U-Net sharpens segmentation outputs by copying encoder feature maps into the decoder through skip connections.

kp_catalog.name: Semantic segmentation as per-pixel prediction
kp_catalog.description: Semantic segmentation labels each pixel with a class label, turning structured scene understanding into dense classification.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because semantic segmentation as per-pixel prediction formalizes how semantic segmentation labels each pixel with a class label, turning structured scene understanding into dense classification, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Fully convolutional segmentation and per-pixel loss
kp_catalog.description: FCN-style segmentation predicts an entire label map with convolutional encoder-decoder structure and is trained by aggregating classification losses across pixels.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because fully convolutional segmentation and per-pixel loss formalizes how fCN-style segmentation predicts an entire label map with convolutional encoder-decoder structure and is trained by aggregating classification losses across pixels, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Upsampling and U-Net skip connections
kp_catalog.description: Decoder-side upsampling can use unpooling or transposed convolution, and U-Net improves dense prediction by copying encoder features into the decoder.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because upsampling and U-Net skip connections clarifies decoder-side upsampling can use unpooling or transposed convolution, and U-Net improves dense prediction by copying encoder features into the decoder, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
